questions.question: Which of the following is a type of similarity-based learning method?
questions.explanation: K-Nearest Neighbor is a type of similarity-based learning method that makes predictions based on the majority vote of the k nearest neighbors.

questions.question: What is the main difference between the Nearest-Neighbor algorithm and the Weighted K-Nearest-Neighbor algorithm?
options: A) Weighted K-Nearest-Neighbor uses a weighted sum of the distances
B) Weighted K-Nearest-Neighbor uses a simple sum of the distances
C) Weighted K-Nearest-Neighbor uses only the distances
D) Weighted K-Nearest-Neighbor uses only the predictions
questions.explanation: The main difference is that the Weighted K-Nearest-Neighbor algorithm gives more importance to the nearest neighbors by using a weighted sum of their distances.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'voting' step in the Weighted K-Nearest-Neighbor algorithm?
options: A) To calculate the distance between the data point and the center of the class
B) To calculate the weighted sum of the distances
C) To determine the class that the data point belongs to
D) To determine the nearest neighbor
questions.explanation: The 'voting' step is used to determine the class that the data point belongs to by counting the number of votes from each class.

questions.question: Which of the following is a disadvantage of the K-Nearest Neighbor algorithm?
questions.explanation: One disadvantage of the K-Nearest Neighbor algorithm is that it can be computationally expensive, especially for high-dimensional data.

questions.question: What is the advantage of using the Weighted K-Nearest-Neighbor algorithm over the K-Nearest Neighbor algorithm?
questions.explanation: One advantage of the Weighted K-Nearest-Neighbor algorithm is that it can improve the accuracy of predictions by giving more importance to the nearest neighbors.
